pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 15 and export_result.sh line 6 confirm this directory path is used consistently throughout the task

Pacifico is confirmed as a real Google Font available at https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Pacifico. Referenced in export_result.sh line 17 as pacifico.xml and in verifier.py line 78 checking for @font/pacifico reference
Task description explicitly specifies this style name, and verifier.py line 77 searches for 'QuoteTextStyle' in the combined styles content

24sp is a valid Android text size using scale-independent pixels (sp), the recommended unit for text sizes per Android Developers documentation at https://developer.android.com/training/multiscreen/screendensities. Verified in task description and checked in verifier.py line 79

Verified as the correct provider authority for Google Play Services downloadable fonts per https://developers.google.com/fonts/docs/android and https://developer.android.com/develop/ui/views/text-and-emoji/downloadable-fonts. Referenced in verifier.py lines 56 and 65

privileged_info_summary: The task implements downloadable fonts in an Android app called QuoteApp. Pacifico is a real, freely-available Google Font from Google Fonts. The provider authority 'com.google.android.gms.fonts' is the correct and standard authority for downloading fonts through Google Play Services in Android. The text size '24sp' is the recommended Android unit for scalable, accessible text sizes. All metadata claims reference valid Android configurations for implementing downloadable fonts. The project structure is synthetically generated during setup.
